core_concepts: Integration with GitHub and GitLab via webhooks
Customizable review rules and prompt templates
Incremental diff analysis for pull requests
supported_claims: Supports both GitHub and GitLab as code hosting platforms, enabling automated review on pull/merge requests (source: README.md).
Allows users to define custom review rules using YAML configuration files, tailoring checks to project-specific standards (source: docs/configuration.md).
Integrates with multiple LLM backends including OpenAI GPT-4, Anthropic Claude, and local models via Ollama, providing flexibility in cost and privacy (source: docs/backends.md).
Performs incremental analysis on diffs rather than full file scans, reducing API costs and latency for large repositories (source: architecture.md).
Open-source under Apache 2.0 license, encouraging community contributions and self-hosting (source: LICENSE file).
common_pitfalls: Over-reliance on AI reviews without human oversight can miss context-specific logic errors or business rules.
Misconfiguration of prompt templates may lead to overly verbose or irrelevant feedback, reducing developer adoption.
Using expensive LLM backends (e.g., GPT-4) on every commit can incur high costs; failing to set rate limits or caching exacerbates this.
Inadequate handling of large diffs or binary files may cause timeouts or incomplete reviews.
Assuming the tool works out-of-the-box without tuning review rules to the project's coding style and conventions.
research_questions: How does open-code-review compare to GitHub's native Copilot Code Review in terms of accuracy and customization?
What are the best practices for designing prompt templates to minimize false positives in code review?
How does the tool handle security-sensitive code (e.g., credentials, encryption) to avoid leaking secrets to external LLM APIs?
What performance benchmarks exist for incremental diff analysis versus full-file review across different repository sizes?
Can the tool be extended to support other CI/CD platforms (e.g., Jenkins, GitLab CI) beyond webhook-based triggers?
success_criteria: Reduction in human review time by at least 30% for routine pull requests without increasing defect escape rate.
Positive developer feedback on relevance and actionability of AI-generated comments (survey score >4/5).
Successful integration with at least two major code hosting platforms (GitHub, GitLab) in a production environment.
Cost per review stays below $0.01 when using a local LLM (e.g., Llama 3 8B) via Ollama.
Zero security incidents related to accidental exposure of proprietary code to external LLM providers over a 3-month trial.

sources.title: Configuration Documentation - Custom Rules and Prompts
